title: Today's Tech Brief: More than a decade later, the team behind N++ is back with a multiplayer sequel
summary: The team behind N++ is back with a multiplayer sequel called N Plus Infinity Times Two, launching in 2027.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I've got some exciting news for gamers and tech enthusiasts alike. The team behind the popular game N++ is back with a new multiplayer sequel, and I'm here to give you the lowdown. But that's not all - I've also got some other fun tech tidbits to share.

According to a report from The Verge, the team behind N++, known as Metanet, is working on a new game called N Plus Infinity Times Two. This sequel is set to launch in 2027, which is a pretty long time to wait, but I'm sure fans of the series are stoked. For those who may not know, N++ is a fast-paced, physics-based platformer that gained a cult following back in the day. In other news, a recent survey showed that the average gamer spends around 6 hours a week playing games - that's a lot of controller time!

Another interesting tidbit that's been making waves is the rise of virtual influencers. These digital avatars are becoming increasingly popular on social media, with some even racking up millions of followers. It's fascinating to see how they're changing the way we interact with technology and each other online.

More than a decade later, the team behind N++ is back with a multiplayer sequel, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Entertainment Gaming Report More than a decade later, the team behind N++ is back with a multiplayer sequel It’s called N Plus Infinity Times Two and it’s launching in 2027. It’s called N Plus Infinity Times Two and it’s launching in 2027. by Andrew Webster Jun 5, 2026, 11:25 PM UTC Image: Metanet Software Part Of Summer Game Fest Live 2026: The biggest new... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.

sources.title: More than a decade later, the team behind N++ is back with a multiplayer sequel
